name: Western Union Money Order Only
description_short: Western Union is a global money transfer service enabling customers to send and receive money internationally and domestically with competitive exchange rates and low fees.
description_long:
Western Union is one of the world's largest money transfer operators, providing financial services that allow individuals to send and receive funds across borders and within the United States. The company has established a global presence with extensive agent networks and digital platforms to facilitate international remittances and domestic money transfers. Their core business model focuses on enabling customers to move money quickly and affordably, with particular emphasis on international corridors serving diaspora communities and families separated by geography.

Western Union offers online money transfer services with transparent fee structures and real-time exchange rate information. Customers can send money from the United States to over 200 countries and territories, with receivers able to collect funds in local currencies. The platform provides upfront fee disclosure, allowing senders to see exactly what they will pay before completing a transaction. New customers can access promotional rates, including $0 fees on first-time international online transfers and better exchange rates for registered users.

The company distinguishes itself through its extensive global reach and multi-channel accessibility, supporting both online digital transfers and physical agent locations. Western Union provides comprehensive currency pair options, serving major remittance corridors including Mexico, Guatemala, Philippines, India, Colombia, and numerous other destinations. Their exchange rate transparency and tiered fee structure ($1.99 to $0.00 USD) reflect competitive positioning in the money transfer market.

Western Union's service model is straightforward but involves fees and exchange rate markups that consumers should evaluate against alternatives. While the company offers convenience and reliability, particularly for international transfers, the cost structure means this solution is not optimal for every transfer scenario. The platform's user experience appears functional based on available website content, though technical issues (such as the noted Orange wallet unavailability) may occasionally impact service delivery.
address: 310 W Uintah St, Colorado Springs, CO 80905

company_info.headquarters: 310 W Uintah St, Colorado Springs, CO 80905

pros: Transparent fee structure with upfront disclosure ($1.99 to $0.00 USD depending on transfer type)
Global reach to 200+ countries and territories with local currency delivery options
Real-time exchange rate information displayed before transaction completion
Promotional incentives for new users including $0 fees on first international online transfer
Better exchange rates available for registered customers versus unregistered users
Online digital transfer option for convenience without requiring physical agent location visits
Multiple currency pair options supporting major remittance corridors (Mexico, Philippines, India, Colombia, etc.)
cons: Exchange rate markups applied to all transactions (rates displayed show spread from 16.9486 to 17.6326 MXN per USD)
Service outages and temporary unavailability of features (Orange wallet noted as temporarily unavailable on website)
Fees apply to most transactions, making this more expensive than some digital-native alternatives for certain transfer types
Website interface appears complex with extensive country/currency dropdown lists that may confuse users unfamiliar with international transfers
best_for: International remittance senders supporting family members in Latin America, Asia, or Africa
Individuals without access to traditional banking services needing reliable money transfer infrastructure
Customers prioritizing established brand recognition and global availability over lowest-cost alternatives
Users sending money to 200+ countries where Western Union has established agent networks
diagnosis: Western Union is best for international remittance senders who prioritize global reach, reliability, and established infrastructure over absolute lowest costs. The main caveat is that fees and exchange rate markups make this a relatively expensive option compared to emerging digital money transfer services, so cost-conscious users should compare alternatives before committing to a transfer.
services: Online international money transfers from United States
Multi-country currency exchange with real-time rate quoting
Cash pickup at agent locations worldwide
Bank deposit delivery to receiver accounts
New customer promotional programs with reduced fees
Registered user account services with improved exchange rates
